examples: A tree, gloriously lit by autumn, pressed against the windowpane.
With vivid brass bellpulls, a poor man might walk here as a Dickensian child put its face to a windowpane.
Sasha looked at the windowpane, rinsed with rain, smearing lights in the falling dark.
It is what an outsider from the street observes when looking through the windowpane of someone else's life.
